title: ageratina molinae
intro: Ageratina molinae, also known as Cuatle or Mexican Ague Tree, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic activities. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and discomfort, and acting as an antioxidant. Ageratina molinae can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
